entries.answer:
Here are the steps for conducting a router speed test:

**Step 1:** Select the "Equipment" tile from the Home page.
**Step 2:** Select the "Router speed test" option towards the top of the Router Details page.
**Step 3:** Select "Start speed test".

aliases: Turn off and on again router
entries.answer:
To restart your router:

**Step 1:** Select the "Equipment" tile from the Home page.
**Step 2:** Select the "Restart router" option towards the top of the Router Details page.
**Step 3:** Select "Restart router".

Please note that all devices connected to the internet through this router will temporarily lose their connection.

Here's how to connect a device through WPS:

**Step 1:** Select the "Equipment" tile from the Home page.
**Step 2:** Select the "WPS (Wi-Fi protected setup)" option towards the top of the Router Details page.
**Step 3:** If WPS is not enabled, enable WPS using the toggle towards the top-right of the page.
**Step 4:** Select the "Start WPS" button at the bottom of the page.

Please note that all devices connected to the internet through this router will temporarily lose their connection.

Follow these instructions to backup your router settings:

**Step 1:** Select the "Equipment" tile from the Home page.
**Step 2:** Select the "Save settings" option on the Router Details page.
**Step 3:** Select the "Manual save" button.

If you would like your router to back up its setting automatically every 7 days, select the "Automatic" toggle.

Here's how you can restore your router settings:

**Step 1:** Select the "Equipment" tile from the Home page.
**Step 2:** Choose the "Restore or reset settings" option on the Router Details page.
**Step 3:** Select which backup you would like to restore your settings from.
**Step 4:** Select the "Continue" button at the bottom of the page.

Please note that if your networks and passwords were different in this backup that all devices connected through your router will need to be reconnected.

Here's how you can reset your router settings:

**Step 1:** Select the "Equipment" tile from the Home page.
**Step 2:** Choose the "Restore or reset settings" option on the Router Details page.
**Step 3:** Select whether you would like to maintain your network names and passwords or erase all settings.
**Step 4:** Select the "Continue" button at the bottom of the page.

Please note that if you erase all of your router settings, all devices connected through the router will need to be reconnected.

Follow these instructions to change your LED settings:

**Step 1:** Select the "Equipment" tile from the Home page.
**Step 2:** Select the "LED brightness" option on the Router Details page.
**Step 3:** Choose which brightness level you would like your Router to have.
**Step 4:** Select "Save" at the bottom of the page.

You can find your router model by:

**Step 1:** Select the "Equipment" tile from the Home page.
**Step 2:** Scroll to the bottom of the page.
**Step 3:** Your router model number is contained in the "Technical information" section.

Here's where you can find your Ipv4 address:

**Step 1:** Select the "Equipment" tile from the Home page.
**Step 2:** Scroll to the bottom of the page.
**Step 3:** Your Ipv4 address is contained in the "Technical information" section.

To find your Ipv 6 address follow these instructions:

**Step 1:** Select the "Equipment" tile from the Home page.
**Step 2:** Scroll to the bottom of the page.
**Step 3:** Your Ipv6 address is contained in the "Technical information" section.

To find your MAC address follow these instructions:

**Step 1:** Select the "Equipment" tile from the Home page.
**Step 2:** Scroll to the bottom of the page.
**Step 3:** Your MAC address is contained in the "Technical information" section.

You can find your firmware version by:

**Step 1:** Select the "Equipment" tile from the Home page.
**Step 2:** Scroll to the bottom of the page.
**Step 3:** Your firmware version is contained in the "Technical information" section.

Here's where you can find your router's serial number:

**Step 1:** Tap on the "Equipment" tile from the Home page.
**Step 2:** Scroll to the bottom of the page.
**Step 3:** Your serial number is contained in the "Technical information" section.

Here's how you can turn on a Wi-Fi network:

**Step 1:** Select the "Network" tile from the Home page.
**Step 2:** Find the Wi-Fi network you'd like to turn on.
**Step 3:** Select the On/Off toggle next to your network's name.

To find your WiFi network name (SSID):

**Step 1:** Select the "Network" tile from the Home page.
**Step 2:** Tap your WiFi network — the name shown at the top is your SSID.
**Step 3:** You can also find the default SSID printed on the sticker on the back or bottom of your router.

The SSID is the public name your devices see when scanning for WiFi.

The Home page is the landing page of a carrier home internet app — it opens automatically once you log in.

**Top of the page:** an AI-generated city/state image of your approximate neighborhood (based on location, not customizable). If you have more than one address on your account, the top-left dropdown lets you switch between them. The top-right button is Notifications.

**Connections section:** three tiles — Equipment, Network, Devices. Each shows a health dot (green = online, amber = unhealthy, grey = offline) and a status label. Tapping a tile takes you to its detail page.

**Quick actions section:** four buttons — Share Wi-Fi, Troubleshoot, Speed Test, Restart Router.

**Digital Secure Home section:**
- If you haven't enrolled, you'll see a description and an "Enroll for $0/mo" CTA.
- If you cancelled a prior subscription, the tile moves to the Discover page.
- If you're actively subscribed, you'll see a Dashboard button plus tiles for "Threats blocked" (last 7 days) and "Vulnerabilities found".

I can check the status of every device on your account in a second:

**Step 1:** Open the Home page and look at the three Connection tiles — Equipment, Network, Devices.
**Step 2:** A green dot means online, amber means degraded, grey means offline.
**Step 3:** Tap any tile for detail on a specific piece of equipment.

Or I can run the check for you now — I'll ping each piece of equipment and report back.

I can run a full network diagnostic from the router: latency, packet loss, DNS resolution, and gateway reachability. It takes about 15 seconds and doesn't interrupt anything connected.

**Step 1:** Tap "Run diagnostics" below.
**Step 2:** Wait for the report — any failing check will be highlighted.
**Step 3:** If something is failing, I'll suggest the next step (restart router, reboot extender, or schedule a technician).

You can manage billing and payments through your carrier app or account portal:

**View your bill:** Log in to the carrier app → select "Bill" from the navigation menu.
**Make a payment:** Select "Pay Bill" and choose your payment method.
**Set up Auto Pay:** Go to Bill → Payment Methods → enroll in Auto Pay for a monthly discount.
**Dispute a charge:** Contact carrier support or chat in the carrier app.

To view or change your home internet plan:

**Step 1:** Open the carrier app or account portal.
**Step 2:** Select your current internet plan to see available options.
**Step 3:** Compare available 300 Mbps, 500 Mbps, and Gigabit speed tiers.
**Step 4:** Select your desired plan and confirm the change.

Plan changes typically take effect on your next billing cycle.

You can manage all devices connected to your network:

**Step 1:** Select the "Devices" tile from the Home page.
**Step 2:** View all connected devices with their status and bandwidth usage.
**Step 3:** Tap any device to see details, rename it, or manage its access.

To remove an unknown device, tap it and select "Block device" to prevent it from reconnecting.

Manage parental controls through the carrier home internet app:

**Step 1:** Select the "Devices" tile from the Home page.
**Step 2:** Tap the device you want to manage.
**Step 3:** Select "Parental Controls" to set content filters, time limits, or pause internet access.

You can also create device profiles for each family member and set schedules for internet access.

Carriers often offer digital security and home network protection features to keep your network safe:

**Digital Secure:** Enroll from the Home page → Digital Secure section. View threats blocked and vulnerabilities found.
**Network Security:** Go to Equipment → Advanced Settings to manage WPA3/WPA2 encryption, hide your SSID, or configure firewall settings.
**If you suspect unauthorized access:** Change your WiFi password immediately via Network → select your network → Edit password.

To check for service outages in your area:

**Step 1:** Open the carrier home internet app — any active outages will appear as a banner on the Home page.
**Step 2:** You can also check the outage map in your carrier portal.
**Step 3:** If an outage is confirmed, estimated restoration times will be displayed when available.

If no outage is reported but your service is down, try restarting your router or running a connection check.

Manage your account settings through the carrier home internet app or account portal:

**Change email/password:** Go to Account → Profile → update your credentials.
**Notification preferences:** Account → Notifications → toggle alerts for outages, billing, and security.
**Move service:** Contact carrier support or use the carrier portal to transfer home internet service to a new address. Start the process at least 2 weeks before your move date.

To change your Wi-Fi password:

**Step 1:** Select the "Network" tile from the Home page.
**Step 2:** Select the Wi-Fi network you'd like to update.
**Step 3:** Tap "Password" and enter your new password.
**Step 4:** Tap "Save".

All devices currently connected will need to reconnect with the new password.

Here's how to share your Wi-Fi with someone in your home:

**Step 1:** From the Home page, tap "Share Wi-Fi" under Quick actions.
**Step 2:** Choose the network you'd like to share (main or guest).
**Step 3:** Show the QR code on your phone — the guest scans it with their camera and joins automatically, no password typing.

You can also tap "Copy password" if they'd rather paste it. For occasional visitors we recommend enabling your Guest network first so the main network password stays private.

Weak signal usually comes from one of a few places:

**1. Distance from the router** — the farther away, the weaker the signal.
**2. Physical barriers** — walls, floors, and appliances block Wi-Fi.
**3. Channel overcrowding** — in dense areas, networks compete for the same channel.
**4. Router placement** — avoid corners, floors, and large appliances.
**5. Interference** — microwaves, cordless phones, and Bluetooth gadgets can all interfere.

**Quick fixes:**
- Power-cycle the router (unplug, 30s, plug back in).
- Move the router to a central, elevated spot.
- For persistent dead zones, a mesh extender or Mesh Wi-Fi pack can help.

If your internet feels slow, try these steps in order:

**Step 1:** Run a router speed test and compare to your plan speed.
**Step 2:** Check your Wi-Fi signal strength — weak signal is the most common cause.
**Step 3:** Power-cycle your router (unplug, 30s, plug back in).
**Step 4:** See if a specific device is slow or all of them — a single slow device usually points to that device, not the network.
**Step 5:** If speeds are consistently below plan, we can look at an equipment swap or a retention credit.

To identify a device on your network:

**Step 1:** Open the Devices tile on the Home page.
**Step 2:** Tap any device to see its name, type (computer / phone / tablet / smart home), connection status, signal strength, and MAC address.
**Step 3:** If you don't recognize it, you can rename it, pause its access, or block it entirely.

If a mesh extender (or mesh node) is dropping or offline:

**Step 1:** Confirm the extender is powered — the status LED should be solid white. Amber or blinking usually means it has lost its backhaul to the main router.
**Step 2:** From the Home page, tap Equipment → select the extender → tap "Restart extender".
**Step 3:** Wait 60 seconds for the extender to rejoin the mesh. The LED should return to solid white.

If it's still offline, try moving the extender closer to the main router — walls and appliances drop backhaul strength quickly. If you've had to restart it more than once this week, schedule a technician visit.

To get the most out of a mesh extender:

**1. Halfway between the router and the dead zone.** Too far and backhaul drops; too close and you're not extending much coverage.
**2. Elevated and unobstructed.** On a shelf, not in a cabinet or behind a TV. Wi-Fi is line-of-sight — every wall and large appliance cuts range.
**3. Away from interference.** Keep at least a few feet away from microwaves, cordless phones, and baby monitors.
**4. One floor at a time.** For multi-story homes, an extender per floor usually beats a single powerful one downstairs.

After repositioning, wait ~60 seconds for the mesh to re-optimize, then run a speed test from the dead zone to confirm.

If you see a duplicate charge on your carrier bill:

**Step 1:** Open the carrier app → Bill → Payment history. Confirm the two transactions have different transaction IDs (a single charge showing twice in pending vs posted is normal and will reconcile within 1-2 business days).
**Step 2:** If both IDs are unique, the fastest resolution is chat: open Billing → Contact us → Chat. Reference both transaction IDs.
**Step 3:** For urgent disputes over $100, contact carrier support. Keep the transaction IDs handy.

Disputed charges are typically credited within 1-2 billing cycles.

If TV service is frozen or won't load:

**Step 1:** Check the Service tile on the Home page — a red dot indicates a known outage.
**Step 2:** If service looks up, power-cycle the set-top box: unplug, wait 30 seconds, plug back in.
**Step 3:** Verify the HDMI cable is seated in both the TV and the box.
**Step 4:** If only streaming apps are affected, confirm the router is online and the TV has strong Wi-Fi signal.
